question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit shouldRetry() which does not check for ALREADY_EXISTS or ABORTED gRPC status codes, causing the system to retry subscription attempts that were rejected due to duplicate or conflicting state, effectively re-authorizing subscriptions that should have been permanently blocked?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user call getSubscriptions() to obtain the cached Flux<GrpcSubscription> and subscribe to it directly, receiving all GrpcSubscription objects including their topicId, startTime, and limit values, enabling the attacker to replicate subscription parameters for unauthorized access to the same topics?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the expressionConverter.convert() call with a topicId expression that references environment variables or system properties, causing the converter to resolve sensitive configuration values and use them to authorize subscriptions to topics without the owner's knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a topic admin key check in clientSubscribe() before calling grpcClient.subscribe(subscription), causing the system to subscribe to a topic that has an admin key restriction without providing the required admin key signature, bypassing topic ownership controls?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux caching in subscriptions = Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache(), causing the first caller to poison the cache with attacker-controlled subscription parameters, and all subsequent callers to receive and execute those unauthorized subscriptions without re-authorization?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any check that the topicId resolved by expressionConverter.convert() is a valid Hedera topic ID format before calling properties.setTopicId(topicId), causing the system to attempt to authorize a subscription to a malformed or attacker-controlled topic identifier?
